description_long: This object is a large, cartoonish ham steak with a prominent bone protruding from one end and a crosshatched, brown exterior rind. The cut face reveals a marbled interior with shades of red and white, simulating the appearance of cured meat with visible fat streaks. The bone is rounded and pale, contrasting with the rich, meaty colors of the steak. The steak is sliced, with two thick, round pieces lying adjacent to the main body, displaying the marbling and texture of the interior. The surface of the rind is smooth with a subtle sheen, and the crosshatch pattern suggests a smoked or cured preparation. There are no visible garnishes, sauces, or decorative elements accompanying the steak.
description: A large, cartoon-style ham steak with a crosshatched brown rind, exposed marbled interior, and a protruding bone, accompanied by two thick slices.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the ham steak, the protruding bone, and two thick slices cut from the main body.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the circular, marbled cut face of the steak, the central bone, and the stacked slices.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the brown rind, bone, and the exposed marbled interior of the cut slices.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the bone and the textured brown rind, with no interior visible.
attribute_grill_marks: There are faint crosshatch grill marks visible on the brown rind, but they are not pronounced.
attribute_visible_fat_marbling: Moderate fat marbling is visible, with streaks of white fat evenly distributed throughout the interior.
